title: how can i run podman in termux with proot?
body:
### Feature request description

Termux is an android app which make android run linux possible.
Now using proot, i have an ubuntu environment. 
```
#> uname -a
# Linux localhost 6.2.1-PRoot-Distro #1 SMP PREEMPT Wed Jun 5 14:47:35 UTC 2024 aarch64 aarch64 aarch64 GNU/Linux
#>  apt install podman
#>  podman version
# WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
# cannot clone: Invalid argument
# Error: cannot re-exec process
```
There is an error, when i use podman. if it possible that i can run container on Android with termux.

### Suggest potential solution

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: I know i can use qemu, but it is to low. It need 290 seconds to power on and 90 seconds to power off.
unfortunately you can't do that.  The environment created by proot is not powerful enough to run Podman
